title: aloe divaricata subsp. vaotsohy
intro: Aloe divaricata subsp. vaotsohy is a lesser-known subspecies of the Aloe genus, native to Madagascar, characterized by its striking yellow or orange flowers and thick, fleshy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin, which are attributed to the presence of aloin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aloe divaricata subsp. vaotsohy include wound healing, skin protection, and relief from digestive issues, such as constipation and ulcers. Aloe divaricata subsp. vaotsohy can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including gel, juice, and powder, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
